description: host-subject DN matching fixtures — compare design algorithm vs spice-gtk/ssl_verify.c
spice_gtk_algorithm: Parse host-subject string TYPE=value pairs split on unescaped commas (\, and \\ escapes).
Build OpenSSL X509_NAME via X509_NAME_add_entry_by_txt(MBSTRING_UTF8) for each RDN in order.
Match with X509_NAME_cmp(cert_subject, expected) == 0 (order-sensitive, all RDNs).
Chain verification still uses SSL verify callback; subject pin is additional check on leaf.
design_algorithm_phase1: Compare CN (case-insensitive fold), O and OU string slices (order-independent multi-value).
Optionally C, ST, L if present in expected.
Do NOT use pkix.Name.String() equality against the .vv host-subject string.
intentional_differences: spice-gtk requires exact RDN count and X509_NAME_cmp order; design is a subset field match (CN/O/OU).

Risk: cert with extra RDNs (emailAddress, ST, L) may pass spice-gtk only if host-subject includes them; design subset match is more permissive on extra cert RDNs but must still require all expected fields.
Phase 1 recommendation: implement design subset match first; add full X509_NAME_cmp parity test if Proxmox field fails in lab.
Proxmox generates host-subject via X509_NAME_oneline then rewrites / to commas (AccessControl::read_x509_subject_spice) — typically includes all subject RDNs from pve-ssl.pem.

fixtures.name: pve_with_geo_and_email
fixtures.comment: Shape from public Proxmox forum .vv example (sanitized)

fixtures.name: extra_rdn_on_cert_only
fixtures.comment: Documents design vs spice-gtk divergence: design subset match ignores extra cert RDNs; spice-gtk entry_count mismatch rejects

fixtures.name: rdn_order_swapped_in_host_subject
fixtures.comment: X509_NAME_cmp is order-sensitive; design field compare is order-independent

fixtures.name: missing_expected_rdn_on_cert
fixtures.comment: host-subject requires OU that cert lacks; design subset-match and spice-gtk both reject (guards against overfitting the permissive extra-on-cert case)
